title: [lichen_l2.c:846] lichen_link_tx called without checking frame_len output parameter
description:
Completeness: Output parameter validation category.

At line 845-846:
ret = lichen_link_tx(&link_ctx, tx_ipv6_buf, pkt_len, NULL, tx_frame_buf, &frame_len);

Before the call, frame_len = sizeof(tx_frame_buf) (line 831).
After the call, frame_len contains the actual frame size.

The validation at lines 855-859 checks frame_len > sizeof(tx_frame_buf), which catches overflow.

However, there's no check for frame_len == 0. If lichen_link_tx() succeeds (ret == 0) but sets frame_len = 0 due to a bug, the subsequent lora_send at line 864 would send an empty frame.

While lichen_link_tx() should never return success with zero frame_len, defensive code should check: if (frame_len == 0) { LOG_ERR(..); return -EINVAL; }

This pairs with the existing underflow check already filed for the RX path.
close_reason: Added frame_len == 0 check before TX to prevent sending empty frames
